name: Lacuna de Gallocanta - circular by the Lacuna de Gallocanta Natural Reserve
name_original: Lacuna de Gallocanta - circular por la Reserva Natural de la Lacuna de Gallocanta
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Gallocanta are characterized by the unique landscape of the Laguna de Gallocanta, the largest natural saline lagoon in the Iberian Peninsula. The region features wide horizons and smooth plains, set within the arid Aragonese steppe at an altitude of 1,000 meters. Cyclists navigate a network of quiet secondary roads, encountering relatively flat terrain around the lagoon interspersed with gentle inclines. Best road cycling routes around Gallocanta The most popular road cycling route is Lacuna de Gallocanta - circular by the Lacuna de Gallocanta Natural Reserve, a 22.9 miles (36.8 km) trail that takes 1 hour 27 minutes to complete. This easy route offers views of the natural reserve and is ideal for birdwatching. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Guided Tour of the Gallocanta Lagoon Natural Reserve - circular from Bello, an easy 22.8 miles (36.7 km) path. This route provides a circular experience around the lagoon, starting from the village of Bello. Local road cyclists also love the Daroca – Calamocha loop from Tornos, a 44.9 miles (72.3 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 3 hours 23 minutes. Road cycling around Gallocanta is defined by the expansive Laguna de Gallocanta, the surrounding arid steppe, and quiet secondary roads. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops around the lagoon to more challenging routes connecting historical villages. The routes in Gallocanta are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.0 stars from over 1 reviews. More than 35 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Gallocanta's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What is the general terrain like for road cycling around Gallocanta?
A: The Gallocanta region offers a unique road cycling experience characterized by wide horizons and smooth plains around the Laguna de Gallocanta, set within the arid Aragonese steppe at an altitude of 1,000 meters. While the terrain around the lagoon is relatively flat, routes also feature gentle inclines and rewarding descents, especially when connecting to historical villages or crossing the Santa Cruz Mountain Range.

Q: What is the typical road surface quality for cycling routes in Gallocanta?
A: Road cycling in Gallocanta primarily uses an extensive network of quiet, low-traffic secondary roads. While some stretches might have poorer road surfaces, the routes are generally accessible and enjoyable for cyclists of average fitness.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes around the Laguna de Gallocanta?
A: Yes, Gallocanta is well-known for its circular routes around the lagoon. Two popular options include the easy &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e963808276/lacuna-de-gallocanta-circular-by-the-lacuna-de-gallocanta-natural-reserve" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lacuna de Gallocanta - circular by the Lacuna de Gallocanta Natural Reserve&lt;/a&gt;, a 36.8 km trail, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e992652458/guided-tour-of-the-gallocanta-lagoon-natural-reserve-circular-from-bello" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guided Tour of the Gallocanta Lagoon Natural Reserve - circular from Bello&lt;/a&gt;, which is 36.7 km and also easy.

Q: What historical sites can I explore while road cycling in Gallocanta?
A: The region is rich in history. You can cycle through charming villages like Bello, Tornos, Berrueco, and Gallocanta itself, which feature hermitages, castles, and Celtiberian remains. Daroca, a monumental city with medieval walls, Mudejar churches, and fortified portals, is also a popular starting point for routes. Consider the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31465870" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de Gallocanta – Anento Castle loop from Tornos&lt;/a&gt; to visit historical points of interest.

Q: What are the best viewpoints or places for wildlife spotting along the road cycling routes?
A: Gallocanta is an internationally recognized bird paradise, especially for migrating cranes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2104442" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gallocanta Lagoon&lt;/a&gt; itself offers spectacular views and birdwatching opportunities. You can visit observatories like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5302514" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Reguera Observatory (Gallocanta Lagoon)&lt;/a&gt; or the Interpretation Center between Bello and Tornos. The Berrueco castle also provides magnificent panoramic views of the lagoon.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Gallocanta?
A: The region is particularly vibrant during the crane migration season, typically from autumn until spring, with peak activity in November and February. This period offers a unique visual and auditory experience for cyclists. However, the relatively flat terrain around the lagoon makes it enjoyable for various skill levels throughout much of the year, weather permitting.

Q: Are there any challenging road cycling routes for experienced cyclists?
A: Yes, Gallocanta offers routes for more experienced cyclists. For example, the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38150365" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Daroca – Laguna de Gallocanta loop from Used&lt;/a&gt; covers 82 km with over 1000 meters of elevation gain, offering a significant challenge. The region also integrates into broader cycling tourism routes like the Camino del Cid, which provides longer journeys.

Q: Where can I find places to eat or stay along the cycling routes?
A: Small villages throughout the Gallocanta region, such as Bello, Tornos, and Gallocanta itself, offer bars, rural lodgings, and places to recharge. Daroca, a larger town, also provides cafes and restaurants, making it a convenient stop or starting point for your cycling adventures.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Gallocanta?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.0 stars. Reviewers often praise the unique natural beauty of the Laguna de Gallocanta, the quiet secondary roads, and the opportunity to combine cycling with birdwatching and exploring historical villages.

Q: Can I access road cycling routes in Gallocanta using public transport?
A: While the region is known for its quiet secondary roads, specific public transport access points directly to trailheads are not extensively detailed. Daroca is a significant town in the area and a popular starting point, which may offer better public transport connections compared to smaller villages.

Q: Are there any longer road cycling routes that connect different towns?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38149466" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Daroca – Calamocha loop from Tornos&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 72.3 km route that leads through varied terrain, connecting these towns. Additionally, the Gallocanta region is integrated into broader cycling tourism routes linked to the Camino del Cid, such as "The Three Taifas" and "The Borderlands," offering multi-day journeys for those looking to explore further.
tips: Gallocanta and its lagoon is one of the most important nesting sites for migratory birds in southern Europe. The cranes, for example, are a very famous species in this area where they take advantage of the waters of this protected space to live.
